mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust economic health and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive revisions in earnings expectations and driving equity prices higher over the medium term.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the headline consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) accelerates rapidly, it forces the central bank to consider restrictive monetary policy. The anticipation of higher future interest rates increases the discount rate on future earnings, causing a negative re-pricing of equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event over a 10-year lookback period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a stagflationary shock. This combination compresses corporate profit margins via lower revenues and sticky labor costs, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3m change in INDPRO < -0.5% AND 3m change in UNRATE > +0.2%
failure_condition: The return profile under the dual-trigger condition is not statistically more negative (t-test p > 0.05) than under the UNRATE trigger alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest-rate regime, accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer confidence and forward-looking demand, which fuels broader economic growth and supports higher equity valuations. This mechanism weakens when monetary policy is tight.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST momentum and forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive during historical periods where FEDFUNDS was below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls to a very low level, it signals maximum labor market tightness. This leads to accelerating wage growth, which compresses corporate margins and prompts the central bank to tighten policy, both of which are negative for equities.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when UNRATE is below 5.2% is not statistically lower than the average return when it is above 5.2%.

mechanism_narrative: High-frequency changes in retail sales (RSAFS) provide a timely read on the strength of the consumer. A positive surprise or strong acceleration in RSAFS leads to immediate upward revisions of revenue forecasts, causing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically greater than zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates an accommodative liquidity environment, reducing the cost of capital for firms and encouraging risk-taking among investors. This 'zero-bound' effect provides a persistent tailwind for equity prices.
failure_condition: The Sharpe ratio of a long-only S&P 500 strategy is not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% compared to when it is above this threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in both real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based and severe collapse in aggregate demand. This joint signal precedes significant downward earnings revisions and triggers a risk-off rotation out of equities.
candidate_trigger: Quarterly GDPC1 growth < 1.0% (annualized) AND 3m avg RSAFS growth < 0.1%
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of correct negative predictions) of the combined signal is not greater than 60% over a full business cycle.
